text.title: tailwind Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tailwind as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agents mix v3 tailwind.config.ts and v4 @theme block in the same project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: v4 ignores tailwind.config.ts; v3 ignores @theme. Specify the major version in every agent prompt. Check tailwindcss package version before generating config.

failure-mode.description: Agents install @tailwindcss/forms but forget `strategy: 'class'`, breaking existing inputs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without `strategy: 'class'`, the forms plugin applies global input resets that override component styles. The strategy must match the project's existing form styling approach.

failure-mode.description: Agents write `@apply` chains with 8–15 utilities in a global stylesheet.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Long @apply chains recreate the BEM stylesheet maintenance problem. Anything needing more than 3 utilities is a component, not a utility alias.

failure-mode.description: After adding a new template file (Blade, Django, ERB), agents forget to update the content glob.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: JIT only scans files matching content glob patterns. New template types silently produce unstyled output until the config is updated.
